channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: When Is The MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Review Embargo Date? MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Review Countdown
item.content:encoded:
Years of work have gone into MOUSE: P.I. For Hire, one of the most imaginative and kooky shooters in the genre. Its style sets it apart from so many that have come before it, and if you need some review validation before buying it, we’ve got embargo dates and times to check out.

Fumi Games might well have a big winner on its hands with MOUSE: P.I. For Hire. Troy Baker assumes the lead role, everyone loves cartoons, making its vintage art style immediately a winner, and it seemingly embraces the best of the best from first-person shooters.

That said, many of you are gunning for the MOUSE: P.I. For Hire review embargo. We can duly oblige you.

MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Review Embargo Date, Explained

The MOUSE: P.I. For Hire review embargo is April 14, giving you two days to catch up on everyone’s reviews two days before the full launch.

Review embargoes typically occur between one and seven days before a game’s launch. MOUSE is giving you a couple of days to get an idea of what the game’s like before committing to a pre-purchase.

  • More Insider Gaming: All MOUSE: PI For Hire Console Platforms & Release, Explained

Confirmation of the review embargo came from none other than the MOUSE: P.I. For Hire X page! So you can lock the date in, and it also helps to know the specific time the embargo lifts.

MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Review Embargo Release Times

8 AM PDT is when the MOUSE: P.I. For Hire embargo lifts on April 14.

US folks will be waking up to the looming MOUSE review embargo, whereas the rest of the world will find out intermittently: UK and European users finally get to see the scores before sitting down for tea.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is MOUSE: PI For Hire On Xbox Game Pass?

For an accurate breakdown of when the embargo lifts around the world, check out this handy table we’ve whipped up.

Make sure you visit the free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord to discuss MOUSE. There are also new, general gaming conversations going on every day.

For more MOUSE: P. I For Hire answers, we have many more guides to check out:

  • All MOUSE: PI For Hire Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices
  • Does MOUSE: PI For Hire Have A Physical Edition? Physical Copy And Disc, Explained
  • Does MOUSE: P.I. For Hire Have Early Access? Demo Status

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: City 20’s Bunker Update Pushes The Apocalypse Underground
item.content:encoded:
City 20 is a unique survival sandbox title that launched into early access back in 2024. It’s a dystopian, unforgiving world that presents impressive levels of freedom to the average player willing to dig through the game’s layers. In the game’s most recent update, the ‘Bunker Update’, those layers have been expanded, and now, players are heading underground.

The Bunker Update introduces several underground areas to the wasteland of a city that players call home in this open-ended title. Additionally, the team at Untold Games has expanded the game’s lore and overhauled many existing areas, making the world more immersive than ever.

City 20 Continues to Evolve in Early Access

In the Bunker Update, City 20 fans will finally get to head deep underground, as nine new areas are being introduced by Untold Games, all of which are bursting with ‘loot and secrets.’

This includes:

  • Lab 01
  • Statistics Agency
  • Anti-Atomic Raboch Bunker
  • Mausoleum

Many of the game’s core areas have been overhauled and rebalanced, with Untold Games addressing spawning conditions, AI behavior, and interior layouts.

Additionally, players will get their hands on new gadgets, tools, and mechanics, namely the new GPS Gadget that adds a minimap and NPC tracking. Talking of NPCs, Untold has tweaked some of the animations and behaviors of the many unique NPCs that occupy City 20.

The Bunker Update also adds many quality-of-life improvements. For instance, the player camera has been updated to be more fluid, interacting with stoves, campfires, and furnaces has been enhanced, and several UI and HUD changes have made the game more intuitive and transparent.

If you’re on the fence about City 20, here’s what we said about the game in our 2024 review:

It’s not inaccurate to suggest that City 20 has unlimited potential. Players are free to go where they please, build what they want, make friends with who they desire, and kill anyone they cross paths with. It’s not a true apocalypse, but it’s not far off the mark, with players being left to survive in a city walled off from the rest of the world 20 years after a radioactive incident.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if City 20 sounds like your kind of game.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Riot Games Unveil Valorant 2027 Circuit Revamp: VCT Cups, Open Qualifiers, and Partnership Details
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has unveiled the details of its 2027 revamp of the Valorant Champions Tour, drastically opening up the circuit to non-Partnered Teams through open qualifiers.

The revamp features new tournaments and financial incentives, but not everything has changed, as Partnered Teams will continue to exist for 2027-28, but in a somewhat different form.

VCT 2027 Circuit: Qualifiers, Cups, and Global events

Riot will open up Tier 1 Valorant by introducing pathways to events through qualification from 2027 onwards. Open qualifiers will take place before Kickoff and prior to VCT Cups. According to Riot, “teams will be provided with multiple opportunities to qualify.”

Regional splits (Stage 1 & Stage 2) have been replaced by VCT Cups, now labelled as ‘Internationals’ to contrast them from qualifiers, which take place in individual countries.

Each international region (EMEA, China, Americas, Pacific) will have two Cups per year, totalling eight Cups over the four regions.

Kickoff remains for 2027-28, but is also open to qualification, with qualifiers starting in Q4 of the previous year.

The best performing teams at Kickoff and Cups will decide the qualification to VCT Masters and Champions, providing a pathway from open competition to the biggest tournaments in Valorant.

Kickoffs and Cups will be played “on a combination of Riot Studios and bespoke venues,” with a weekend event acting as the climax of the competition.

Qualifiers have no set venues, and can take many forms, including community tournaments, partner events, collegiate events, Premier, and more.

Including the two VCT Masters per year and Valorant Champions, Riot is promising 20 events across 16 cities each year.

Riot outlines financial package for 2027 events

The new circuit comes with new financial rewards for competing teams, with financial rewards stretching beyond partnered teams.

Qualification to Kickoff, Cups, Masters, and Champions will award a cash amount, with the amount doubling from Kickoff/Cups to Masters and doubling again for Champions.

Riot has also announced a $6 million package of prize pools per year associated with events, as well as funding for travel for global events, intended to enable teams to attend events.

Incentive funds for Game Changers will also be available, but have not yet been outlined.

“VCT 2027 is about reimagining how teams compete and how fans experience VALORANT esports. By shifting to a tournament-driven system with open access to our biggest events, we’re creating a more dynamic, high-stakes ecosystem where every match matters and every team has a shot at the global stage.”

Valorant partnership model survives in an altered form

As the current partnership agreements ended at the end of 2026, it was unclear whether the partnership model would persist into 2027 and beyond. We now know Partnered Teams will still exist, but in a somewhat altered form.

Another two-year partnership cycle will run from 2027-28, with Riot accepting applications from existing partnered teams and those who are not already partnered.

Partnership will confer the following benefits in that timeframe:

  • Partner Base Payment: A guaranteed base payment
  • Performance Bonus: A bonus for “teams that excel at partnership goals”
  • Team Capsules: In-game team-themed skins
  • Direct Seeding: Direct placement into later rounds in qualifiers

The majority of the partnership benefits are financial, however the last listed reward shows that there is still a competitive advantage for partnered teams.

As partnership no longer guarantees a place in regional league splits, Stage 1 and Stage 2, the placement in the Cup qualifiers gives a safety net for those partnered teams.

Riot still has plenty to unveil of its 2027-28 Valorant circuit, including dates, locations, and formats for its planned events. Insider Gaming will keep you up to date when they do.

What do you think of the changes? Discuss that on Insider Gaming’s Discord.
For more esports news, see where the Esports Awards 2026 will take place.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
